commands.review_summary:
This PR adds runtime plugin load progress reporting through the plugin loader, embedded-agent, and cron paths, then includes attempted/completed/in-flight plugin details in cron timeout messages.

PR surface: Source +179, Tests +148. Total +327 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main only formats the runtime-plugins phase, and the linked issue provides production examples of that unactionable message. I did not establish a live hung-plugin reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer scope handling for the linked issue, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Make runtime plugin startup stalls name in-flight plugins
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds diagnostic progress snapshots while runtime plugins load.
- Threads runtime plugin progress into embedded-agent and cron execution phase reporting.
- Appends attempted/completed/in-flight plugin ids to cron timeout messages only while the last phase is `runtime_plugins`.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87327

## User-facing Bug

When an isolated cron/agent run stalls while loading runtime plugins, the watchdog currently reports only a coarse phase such as `runtime-plugins`. That does not identify which plugin was being resolved, loaded, or registered, so operators cannot tell which plugin to disable, inspect, or report.

## Exact Repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Arrange runtime plugin loading to report progress for two plugins, with `memory-core` still in `register`.
2. Let the cron pre-execution watchdog time out while the last execution phase is `runtime_plugins`.
3. Before this patch, the timeout text only identified the coarse `runtime-plugins` phase.
4. After this patch, the timeout text includes:

```text
runtime plugins: attempted=[telegram, memory-core]; completed=[telegram]; in-flight=memory-core/register
```

The regression coverage in `src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` exercises this watchdog path.

## Fix Summary

- Added `RuntimePluginLoadProgress` diagnostics types under `src/plugins/runtime/load-diagnostics.ts`.
- Added a loader progress tracker that reports planned plugin ids, completed ids, and the current in-flight plugin phase without letting observer errors affect activation.
- Forwarded the diagnostics through `ensureRuntimePluginsLoaded`, embedded runner phase callbacks, and cron isolated setup.
- Formatted runtime plugin details in cron timeout messages, gated to the `runtime_plugins` phase so stale progress does not pollute later `auth` or `context_engine` timeouts.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron/plugin startup stalls now name attempted, completed, and in-flight runtime plugins in timeout diagnostics.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree on macOS with repository Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/runtime-plugins.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plugins.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-plugins.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/service/execution-errors.test.ts src/cron/service/execution-errors.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/types.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/loader.ts src/plugins/runtime/load-diagnostics.ts
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/runtime-plugins.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-plugins.test.ts src/cron/service/execution-errors.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts
codex review --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix:

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `oxlint` passed on the changed runtime plugin diagnostic files.
- `tsgo` passed for `test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`.
- Focused Vitest passed: bundled config `153` tests, cron config `47` tests, agents config `9` tests.
- The cron timeout regression asserts the timeout includes `attempted`, `completed`, and `in-flight` runtime plugin details.
- Copied terminal output from the local timeout-diagnostics proof includes the user-facing runtime plugin detail:

```text
Error: isolated agent timed out during runtime-plugins
runtime plugins: attempted=[telegram, memory-core]; completed=[telegram]; in-flight=memory-core/register
```

- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable regressions.

Observed result after fix: the timeout text names the in-flight plugin and phase instead of only saying `runtime-plugins`.

What was not tested:

- I did not run `pnpm check:changed`; this PR is covered by focused lint, typecheck, Vitest, and `codex review`.
- I did not run a live gateway with an intentionally hung real plugin; the proof is source-level watchdog/loader coverage.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This PR was implemented with AI assistance. I reviewed the generated diff, ran the targeted tests above, and addressed the actionable `codex review` finding before preparing this PR.
target.author: alexzhu0
